varieties.name: Gulf Gold
varieties.traits: Medium to large size, yellow skin with red blush, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm golden-yellow flesh, juicy, excellent sweet flavor, clingstone, small seed
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, bears heavily even in warm climates
varieties.growing_notes: Very low chill requirement (275 hours), specifically bred for subtropical and coastal regions. Resistant to bacterial spot. Self-pollinating but benefits from Gulf Ruby. Perfect for Brisbane's climate, frost-free sites. First plums ready October. Grows well in half wine barrels.
varieties.availability: Daleys Nursery, Fruit Salad Trees, widely available in Queensland

varieties.name: Gulf Ruby
varieties.traits: Deep red skin, medium to large size, clingstone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm yellow flesh, delicious eaten fresh, sweet and juicy

varieties.name: Gulf Blaze
varieties.traits: Red skin, medium size, early flowering and early ripening
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm yellow flesh, sweet, good texture

varieties.name: Santa Rosa
varieties.traits: Large, purplish-crimson to deep crimson skin with pale blue bloom, freestone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh with red blush near skin, mildly sweet with some tartness near skin and stone, juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer, considered one of the best plums

varieties.name: Mariposa
varieties.traits: Large heart-shaped fruit, dark red to maroon skin with green-khaki lenticels giving spotted effect
varieties.flesh_fruit: Blood red flesh, fine textured, juicy, soft when ripe, sweet flavor with good acid balance
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent bearer, crops well in most Australian regions
varieties.growing_notes: Low chill variety, the 'old blood plum everyone remembers'. Partially self-fertile but benefits from Santa Rosa pollination. Popular for jams and sauces. Suitable for early to mid-season harvest (late January). Regular thinning ensures larger fruit size. Widely recognized heritage variety for subtropical regions.
varieties.availability: Widely available - PlantNet, Yalca, Engalls, Fruit Salad Trees, Fruitopia Nursery Brisbane

varieties.name: Satsuma
varieties.origin: China, introduced to USA in 19th century
varieties.traits: Medium to large rounded fruit, red to dark red skin, freestone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red to purple flesh, tender, sweet and juicy with satisfying clove-like depth of flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer, vigorous growth, may require fruit thinning

varieties.name: Dorrigo Blood
varieties.origin: Australian selection, Daleys Nursery unique variety
varieties.traits: Large fruit, pale skin with reddish tones
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep blood red flesh, sensational flavor, smooth soft texture, very juicy, sweet right to seed, sweet mild skin
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, one of largest blood plums
varieties.growing_notes: Low chill blood plum (300-400 hours), self-pollinating. Superb for cooler climates but will crop in subtropics. Cross-pollinate with Satsuma for best results. Unique to Daleys Nursery. Suitable for Brisbane conditions. Ripens late January-early February.

varieties.name: Amber Jewel
varieties.traits: Large fruit (63-70mm), red skin with yellow background, excellent shelf life
varieties.flesh_fruit: Amber flesh, super sweet, meaty crisp texture, unique flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Medium vigorous tree, large fruit production, high quality
varieties.growing_notes: Matures one month after Santa Rosa. Great storage capability. Partially self-fertile, Santa Rosa is compatible pollinator. Will make people come back for more due to exceptional sweetness. Ripens early February. Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate.

varieties.name: Sunrise Gulf
varieties.traits: Large purple fruits, medium to large red clingstone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale green/yellow flesh, sweet and juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Great quality fruits in warmer areas

varieties.name: A-Okay™
varieties.traits: Modern low chill variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, suitable for fresh eating

varieties.name: Yarrahapinni Blood
varieties.traits: Small to medium size, deep red foliage, striking pink blossoms in spring
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark colored flesh, juicy and sweet

varieties.name: Kyogle Early
varieties.origin: Australian selection, under trial at Daleys Nursery
varieties.traits: Red skin, compact habit tree, early flowering
varieties.flesh_fruit: Gorgeous amber flesh, early ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Low chill plum under trial. First plums of season ready in October in Kyogle (northern NSW). Very early ripening. Compact tree habit ideal for smaller spaces. Being evaluated for subtropical performance.

varieties.name: Black Adder Pluot
varieties.origin: Graham Richards low chill breeding program, Australia
varieties.traits: Plum-apricot hybrid (Pluot), purplish-black skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Amber flesh turning deeper when very ripe, excellent flavor and texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, first fruiting shows promise
varieties.growing_notes: Low chill variety specifically bred for Australian subtropical conditions. Cross between apricot and plum with more plum characteristics. Requires cross-pollination with another Pluot or Japanese plum variety like Mariposa. Australian breeding innovation.

varieties.name: Flavour Supreme Pluot
varieties.traits: Plum-apricot hybrid, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright flavor, very sweet, excellent taste
varieties.growing_notes: One of new generation of traditionally bred plums. Fruits early to mid-summer. Red to deep plum colored skin with mulberry red flesh. Suitable for cool temperate to warm areas. Suggested pollinator Mariposa. May adapt to Brisbane with proper care.

varieties.name: Donsworth
varieties.traits: Dark maroon with red/green spots, large freestone, egg-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark red flesh, tart skin but sweet flesh, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, large fruit if thinned
varieties.growing_notes: Old-fashioned blood plum. Stores and handles well - Taiwan market favorite after 16 days sea freight. Harvested mid-January. Pollinates with Narrabeen and Mariposa. Wide climate range adaptability including Brisbane.

varieties.name: Queen Rosa
varieties.traits: Nice red color, good size, old-fashioned plum smell
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, sweet with slight tart skin
varieties.growing_notes: Harvested early January. Classic plum characteristics with nostalgic appeal. Pollinates with Satsuma. Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate.

varieties.name: Rose Zee
varieties.traits: Red color, smallish fruit compared to others but early
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet yellow flesh

varieties.name: Black Amber
varieties.traits: Black plum with good size, excellent keeping ability
varieties.flesh_fruit: Amber flesh, sweet

varieties.name: Frontier
varieties.traits: Heart shape, red skin, no tartness in skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Lighter red flesh, sweet to eat
varieties.growing_notes: Blood plum harvested mid-January. Sweet skin without tartness makes it popular for fresh eating. Suitable for Brisbane conditions.

varieties.name: Ruby Blood
varieties.traits: Medium to large freestone, red fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: Blood red flesh, sweet and juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Great cropper, very popular late bearing variety
varieties.growing_notes: Medium-lower chill but not suitable to subtropical climates north of Brisbane. Pollinates with Spring Satin Plumcot, Narrabeen, Satsuma, Santa Rosa. Popular in Australia. Harvest January-February.
varieties.availability: PlantNet Australia (not recommended north of Brisbane)

varieties.name: Narrabeen
varieties.traits: Large pink/red clingstone fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light red flesh, good quality

varieties.name: Elephant Heart
varieties.traits: Large heart-shaped plum, dark red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light red flesh, excellent flavor

varieties.name: Indian Coffee Plum (Flacourtia jangomas) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India and Southeast Asia, indigenous to Kerala
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree 6-10m, thorns on young branches, light brown to copper-red flaky bark
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round berries 25mm diameter, soft flesh with 4-6 flattened seeds, sour tangy flavor, sweet when fully ripe
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant fruit every year in warm tropical climate
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Kerala's warm tropical climate. Known locally as 'Lubikka' in Malayalam. Eaten raw with salt to offset sourness. Used for jams, jellies, juices, preserves, pickles. Famous Lubikka pickles from Kerala exported worldwide. Traditional Ayurvedic medicinal uses. Flowers fragrant, smell like honey. Male and female flowers on separate trees.
varieties.availability: Local Kerala nurseries, Green Cover Initiative, indigenous to region

varieties.name: Jamun / Java Plum (Syzygium cumini) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India, indigenous across Indian subcontinent including Kerala
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree 30-35ft (up to 100ft in native habitat), leathery glossy dark green leaves, dense canopy
varieties.flesh_fruit: Oblong purple-black fruit size of large olive, sweet-sour flavor, refreshing, stains tongue purple, fleshy pulp with pale oblong seed
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - mature trees produce 85-150 lbs fruit. Bears continuously 60-70 years

varieties.name: Governor's Plum (Flacourtia indica) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to tropical Africa and South Asia, traditional in Kerala/Karnataka
varieties.traits: Deciduous shrub or small tree 3-6m tall, thorny branches, ovate serrated leaves, glossy surface
varieties.flesh_fruit: Globose drupes ripen green to bright red-orange, tart fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts to dry tropical regions with well-drained, slightly acidic soils. Prime cultivation zones include Karnataka and Tamil Nadu in India. Traditional Ayurvedic uses. Known as Batokia, Govardhanphalika. Used as digestive aid. Bark harvested pre-monsoon (March-May). Contains tannins, gallic acid, quercetin. Referenced in Kerala 16th century texts, British India archives.
varieties.availability: Kerala traditional medicine suppliers, specialized Ayurvedic nurseries

varieties.name: Rainforest Plum (Eugenia candolleana)
varieties.origin: Native to Atlantic rainforest of Brazil, adapted to Kerala
varieties.traits: Medium-sized shrubby tree 3-6m tall, rounded/conical canopy 2m wide, reddish brown peeling bark
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round or slightly elongated berry 20mm wide x 25mm long, purple-black skin, whitish pulp 3-5mm thick, edible, moderately sweet, jaboticaba-like aroma
varieties.yield_notes: Flowers in large clusters, fruits ripen several weeks later, purple and red fruited forms
varieties.growing_notes: Known locally as Cambuí roxo or Murtinha in Portuguese. Adapted to Kerala's tropical conditions. Leaves rusty-brown initially turning yellow then deep dark green. Rare in wild, used in landscaping for bright green foliage and purple-black fruits. Suitable for Kerala's humid climate.
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala, specialty tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Kala Amritsari
varieties.origin: Amritsar, Punjab, India - famous traditional variety
varieties.traits: Distinctively large size, darker skin compared to other Indian varieties
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellowish flesh, extremely sweet flavor, juicy pulp
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, profuse flowering, high fruit set

varieties.name: Satluj Purple
varieties.origin: Punjab, India - named after Sutlej River
varieties.traits: Dark purple skin with distinctive purplish-blue color, recognizable from distance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very juicy and sweet, excellent for fresh juices and smoothies
varieties.growing_notes: Needs Kala Amritsari for cross-pollination. Low chill requirement suitable for warmer Indian climates. Grown in Punjab where Sutlej is primary river. Famous for juice production. May require adaptation trials in Kerala conditions.

varieties.name: Alucha
varieties.origin: Not native to India but pervasively grown in Himachal Pradesh
varieties.traits: Bright red, sometimes maroonish hues, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Flavors between sweet and sour, contrasting taste
varieties.growing_notes: Name means 'juicy edible fruit' in Hindi. Generally used for chutneys and pickles due to contrasting flavors. Most abundantly available plum in India. Grown in hilly regions. Not suitable for Kerala's lowland tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Himachal Pradesh nurseries, northern hill regions

varieties.name: Golden Japan
varieties.origin: Japan, cultivated in India - Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand
varieties.traits: Golden yellow color, medium-sized, sometimes small yellow dots on surface
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet but not overly sweet, balanced flavor

varieties.name: Santa Rosa (India)
varieties.origin: California USA, 1906, cultivated in India - J&K, Himachal Pradesh
varieties.traits: Purple with red blush, vibrant appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very tender and juicy flesh, sweet with undertone of tartness

varieties.name: Greengage (India cultivation)
varieties.origin: Iran origin, cultivated in Tamil Nadu for Indian market
varieties.traits: Greenish round fruit, not common green color for plums
varieties.flesh_fruit: Flavors similar to ripe mango, dried apricot, citrusy notes, versatile
varieties.growing_notes: Best for confectionery due to versatile flavors. Cultivation in Tamil Nadu. Transported from Tamil Nadu to rest of country. High chill requirement limits Kerala suitability, but Tamil Nadu proximity suggests possible adaptation in Kerala highlands.
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu nurseries, possibly Kerala highland regions

varieties.name: Titron
varieties.origin: Indian selection of Japanese origin (Prunus salicina)
varieties.traits: Low chill requirement variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good taste, best consumed fresh

varieties.name: Kataru Chak
varieties.traits: Low chill requirement variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Suitable for fresh consumption

varieties.name: Indian Hog Plum / Ambarella (Spondias dulcis) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to regions around Indian Ocean, tropical Asia
varieties.traits: Green when unripe, yellow when ripe, oval shaped, large hairy pit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unripe: tangy, very crunchy/hard. Ripe: soft flesh, sweet with acidity, like mix of green mango and pineapple
varieties.growing_notes: Known as June plum. Popular in South India including Kerala. Eaten raw with salt and chili powder or made into pickles and chutneys. Thrives in tropical conditions. Well-adapted to Kerala climate. Listed among main fruits grown in Kerala.
varieties.availability: Kerala nurseries, backyards, widely cultivated in state

varieties.name: Methley
varieties.traits: Japanese type, low chill variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, good quality

varieties.name: Wade
varieties.traits: Japanese type, low chill requirement
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality

varieties.name: Shiro
varieties.traits: Yellow-skinned plum, tender to cold
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet yellow flesh, both sweet and tangy
varieties.yield_notes: Good for fresh eating or baking

varieties.name: Kelsey
varieties.traits: Green plum turning yellow, shows marks easily being green
varieties.flesh_fruit: Lime green flesh going to yellow with odd red blush, very sweet with no acid, can grow quite large
varieties.yield_notes: Large fruit possible, popular at growers markets
